task_completion.description: Agent accessed the VistA database through the web browser or terminal tools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the YDBGui interface with VistA globals, or a terminal window showing vista-query or docker exec commands querying VistA data.

task_completion.description: Agent identified the highest-risk patient by cross-referencing pharmacy and allergy records and computing the risk score formula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output or browser view showing the agent iterating through ^PS(55) and ^GMR(120.8) to count prescriptions and allergies per patient, computing risk scores, and determining the top patient.

task_completion.description: Agent created the patient_risk_dossier.txt file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the file /home/ga/Desktop/patient_risk_dossier.txt being created or written.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains patient demographics: full name, date of birth, and sex from ^DPT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes a demographics section with the patient's full name, a date of birth, and sex/gender.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains resolved pointer values for Period of Service (from ^DIC(21)) and Primary Eligibility (from ^DIC(8)) as human-readable names, not raw internal IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows a service era name (e.g. 'VIETNAM ERA', 'PERSIAN GULF WAR') resolved from ^DIC(21) and an eligibility category name (e.g. 'SC LESS THAN 50%') resolved from ^DIC(8), not just numeric pointer values.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains rated disability information resolved through ^DIC(31), with condition names and percentages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes disability condition names (resolved from ^DIC(31)) and associated percentages, or explicitly states that no rated disabilities were found.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains a complete list of allergy reactant names for the target patient from ^GMR(120.8).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file lists specific drug or substance allergy reactant names extracted from the VistA allergy system.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains a prescription summary with total count and drug names for at least the first 10 prescriptions from ^PSRX.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows the total prescription count and lists individual drug names extracted from ^PSRX records.

task_completion.description: The dossier contains a contraindication screening section that checks whether any allergy reactant appears as a substring in any prescription drug name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes a section explicitly analyzing potential conflicts between documented allergies and prescribed medications, either listing matches found or stating that no matches were found.

integrity.description: The agent queried the actual VistA database to obtain patient data, rather than fabricating or hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively querying VistA globals through YDBGui, terminal commands, or MUMPS scripts, with real database responses visible.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely cross-referenced both the pharmacy system (^PS(55)) and allergy system (^GMR(120.8)) to identify the target patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent accessing both ^PS(55) and ^GMR(120.8) data before determining which patient to profile.

integrity.description: The agent actually navigated pointer chains by looking up values in ^DIC(21), ^DIC(8), and/or ^DIC(31) rather than guessing human-readable names.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent reading pointer values from ^DPT nodes and then navigating to the corresponding ^DIC globals to retrieve the referenced names.

integrity.description: The agent compiled the report from data gathered during the session, not from pre-existing knowledge or hardcoded answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen constructing the output file by combining data retrieved from multiple database queries during the task session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The risk score formula is (prescription_count * 2) + (allergy_count * 3). The patient with the most prescriptions in VEHU is DFN 1 with 208 prescriptions. The dossier must include resolved names from ^DIC(21) (Period of Service), ^DIC(8) (Eligibility), and ^DIC(31) (Disabilities) — raw pointer IDs are not acceptable. The output file must be at /home/ga/Desktop/patient_risk_dossier.txt.
